title: Next
wikitext:
[]: Next...             Quote Post by Hiten777 » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:18 pm Maybe focus on animating some of the manga adaptations, but it'd more profitable to have alternate endings that in the mangas. For instance, having Shinji end up with Rei instead of Asuka in an anime adaptation of Angelic Days. Just an example, but since these manga are based on game adaptations wouldn't it make more sense to appeal to different fanbases at the same time. I mean, Please Twins had different endings the manga had the main character end up with 1 sister while the anime had him end up with the other. See problem solved and more variety, no one really gets excited about seeing to same cenario with characters ending up with the same people in every adaptation, I mean if I was able to predict who was going to end up with who then I'd be more likely to get bored with the plot altogether, only interested in the tiny changes they make to the story. Finally, WHEN r they gonna try an translate Eva games and make them playable in America. Other people want to play them too!!

[]: Re: Next...             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:07 pm Hiten777 wrote: Maybe focus on animating some of the manga adaptations, but it'd more profitable to have alternate endings that in the mangas.

[]: Re: Next...             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Oct 25, 2009 2:26 am Hiten777 wrote: Maybe focus on animating some of the manga adaptations, but it'd more profitable to have alternate endings that in the mangas. For instance, having Shinji end up with Rei instead of Asuka in an anime adaptation of Angelic Days. Just an example, but since these manga are based on game adaptations wouldn't it make more sense to appeal to different fanbases at the same time. I mean, Please Twins had different endings the manga had the main character end up with 1 sister while the anime had him end up with the other. See problem solved and more variety, no one really gets excited about seeing to same cenario with characters ending up with the same people in every adaptation, I mean if I was able to predict who was going to end up with who then I'd be more likely to get bored with the plot altogether, only interested in the tiny changes they make to the story. Finally, WHEN r they gonna try an translate Eva games and make them playable in America. Other people want to play them too!!

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Sun Oct 25, 2009 11:49 am Why would you reanimate a manga (or a non-canon manga for that matter) that's an adaptation of a show that's ALREADY BEEN ANIMATED (Twice to bump)? I don't see the point you're trying to make here. Not to mention a lot of Eva fans hate the manga enough as it is, we don't need it to be glorified into with a third different reincarnation of the show. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Next...             Quote Post by cojax8 » Tue Oct 27, 2009 10:06 pm Hiten777 wrote: Finally, WHEN r they gonna try an translate Eva games and make them playable in America. Other people want to play them too!!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Oct 27, 2009 10:17 pm EDIT: Never mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Oct 27, 2009 11:46 pm I'm a fan of LET IT REST. No need for a Live Action Version, an All CG Version, a Puppet Pal Clem Version, nothing. No need to animate the Giggly Rei Manga or the Shinji fucks Everything Manga or the Kaworu is Not Annoying Manga. No need to reanimate NGE frame by frame to have new bland standardized animation. The series will go on living through merchandise but it'd be nice to see something given a sense of finality. But even THE END of Evangelion couldn't make that happen so I don't know what to expect.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 28, 2009 12:10 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: I'm a fan of LET IT REST. No need for a Live Action Version,.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Wed Oct 28, 2009 12:15 am I would not mind seeing some anime versions of the manga adaptations as long it's not Angelic Days. I do argee with the post above me since I rather have the series take a rest after the Rebuild series (or maybe a live action series if that ever gets made) since the series will go on living through merchandise just like most animes that have ended. translate Eva games and make them playable in America.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Oct 28, 2009 12:40 am Sigh. You can make an original science fiction movie with ideas and challenging moral issues for $30 million. You can't make that same film for $140 million. Live Action Eva would require a budget in the minimum of $130 million. You'd have a Transformers clone. Not Blade Runner for the new millenium, or anything that even resembled NGE in the first place. Let the series rest. Let it exist through merchandise alone.

[]: Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 29, 2009 4:48 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: You can't make that same film for $140 million.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Oct 29, 2009 5:42 am Alaska Slim wrote: I disagree, that's exactly the sort of thing WETA has pledged themselves to doing, and considering their former work I see no reason to doubt them (seperated into three parts, they made each LOTR films on far less).

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 29, 2009 1:27 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: I disagree, that's exactly the sort of thing WETA has pledged themselves to doing, and considering their former work I see no reason to doubt them (seperated into three parts, they made each LOTR films on far less).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 29, 2009 1:42 pm Alaska Slim wrote: I disagree, Evangelion doesn't have the following, but a niche of a dark and brooding Transformer -esce story? I think there is a market for that.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Oct 29, 2009 1:44 pm Alaska Slim wrote: I disagree, Evangelion doesn't have the following, but a niche of a dark and brooding Transformer -esce story? I think there is a market for that. And keep in mind it's a trilogy, which means a bait & switch could occur just as with the series.

[]: Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 29, 2009 2:50 pm NemZ wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: I disagree, Evangelion doesn't have the following, but a niche of a dark and brooding Transformer -esce story? I think there is a market for that.

[]: Re: Bl             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Oct 29, 2009 4:33 pm Alaska Slim wrote: ... so Peter Jackson is what exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 29, 2009 5:40 pm Uh, Peter Jackson himself doesn't have anything to do with LAEM, but Weta (who IIRC helped with Lord of the Rings) does.

[]: Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Oct 29, 2009 8:59 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: ... so Peter Jackson is what exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Oct 30, 2009 12:49 am Peter Jackson has publicly said he's not that into Eva and has no interest in spending several years of his life working on it. This is old news, stuff that was stated back in 2005. Richard Taylor is the only one who's expressed interest but he's a special effects director and that's it. Despite the fan geek love they may have in the public world, an effects guy wanting to do a film does not get it made. As a sidenote, with The Hobbit going into production you're gonna see Richard Taylor too busy to think of anything else for several years. Finally, with ADV essentially bankrupt Matt Greenfield is a non-entity at this point (the man has done zero legitimate work with film studios and has zero control of Eva outside of the animated medium. I'd bet a wager if any contract regarding a Live Action Adaptation was signed between ADV and Gainax- this was pre Khara- the long stay of inactivity has rendered the contract null and void. It's like how 20th Century Fox lost their rights to Watchmen because they did nothing with it for years...and then bitched their way into getting profits a few years later). And can anyone even take Matt Greenfield spearheading a $130 million + project seriously? He's never done any actual production work outside of a few videos no one has seen (I'd like to point out doing dubs of previous productions with the same gallery of 30 actors is not REAL production) and the man is so powerless now he has no hand in the Rebuild films. Right there is a version of Eva closer to NGE than a live action film could. Hell, they're the same medium- Animation- and the guy has no control in it. Matt Greenfield is not going to produce or have a hand in producing any big scale movies anytime soon. Maybe a few local Texas shorts but a Trilogy on par in scale with The Lord of the Rings? Nope. That's a fan wanking pipe dream. Like the kids who are still waiting for a Batman Vs. Superman movie. It ain't gonna be happening anytime soon.
